zh.Dennington, Suffolk, England: 英國英格蘭素福爾可丹寧頓
zh.Westhall, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可維斯塔爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Artys於1570年5月4日在英國素福爾可維斯塔爾受洗時，她的父親Thomas Artys40歲，母親Judith Elizabetha Wylley36歲。她於1590年11月8日與Robert Goodale在英國素福爾可丹寧頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1610年12月在英國英格蘭素福爾可丹寧頓享年40歲。

zh-hans.Dennington, Suffolk, England: 英国英格兰素福爾可丹寧頓
zh-hans.Westhall, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可維斯塔爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Artys于1570年5月4日在英国素福爾可維斯塔爾受洗时，她的父亲Thomas Artys40岁，母亲Judith Elizabetha Wylley36岁。她于1590年11月8日与Robert Goodale在英国素福爾可丹寧頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1610年12月在英国英格兰素福爾可丹寧頓享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Artys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and German: from a medieval variant of the personal name   Arthur  .

variants.name: Arthur
meanings: Scottish, Irish, English, German, and French: from the ancient Celtic personal name Arthur. The origin of the name is disputed, but it may be derived from the Latin name Artorius, or it may derive from an old Celtic word meaning ‘bear’ (compare Gaelic art, Welsh arth, both of which mean ‘bear’). It has been in regular use as a personal name in Britain since the early Middle Ages, owing its popularity in large part to the legendary exploits of King Arthur, which gave rise to a prolific literature in Welsh, French, English, German, and other languages. In many cases this is a shortened form of Scottish or Irish McArthur , the patronymic Mac- often being dropped in the 17th, 18th, and 19th centuries under English influence.
West African (Ghana): Anglicized form of the Akan (Fante) name   Atta  .
History: President Chester Alan Arthur (1830–86) was the son of a VT Baptist preacher, William Arthur (1797–1875), who emigrated from Ballymena in northern Ireland c. 1815.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Artys was christened on 4 May 1570, in Westhall, Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas Artys, was 40 and her mother, Judith Elizabetha Wylley, was 36. She married Robert Goodale on 8 November 1590, in Dennington, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died in December 1610, in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Artys a été baptisée le 4 mai 1570, à Westhall, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Artys, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Judith Elizabetha Wylley, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Goodale le 8 novembre 1590, à Dennington, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en décembre 1610, à Dennington, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Artys am 4. Mai 1570 in Westhall, Suffolk, England getauft wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Artys 40 und ihre Mutter Judith Elizabetha Wylley 36. Sie hat am 8. November 1590 in Dennington, Suffolk, England Robert Goodale geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb im Dezember 1610 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Artys è stato battezzato il 4 maggio 1570 nel Westhall, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Thomas Artys, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Judith Elizabetha Wylley, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato Robert Goodale il 8 novembre 1590, nel Dennington, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel dicembre 1610, nel Dennington, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.Dennington, Suffolk, England: イギリスイギリススフォルクデニントン
ja.Westhall, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクウエスサル
ja.lifeSummary: Joan Artysが1570年5月4日にイギリススフォルクウエスサルで洗礼を受けた時，父Thomas Artysは40歳，母Judith Elizabetha Wylleyは36歳であった。1590年11月8日にイギリススフォルクデニントンでRobert Goodaleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1610年12月にイギリスイギリススフォルクデニントンで40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Artys(이)가 1570년 5월 4일 영국 수폴크 웨스솔에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Artys(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Judith Elizabetha Wylley(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1590년 11월 8일 영국 수폴크 데닝턴에서 Robert Goodale(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1610년 12월 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 데닝턴에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Artys foi batizada em 4 de maio de 1570, em Westhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Artys, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Judith Elizabetha Wylley, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Goodale em 8 de novembro de 1590, em Dennington, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em dezembro de 1610, em Dennington, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Artys была крещена 4 мая 1570 г. в местности вестолл, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Artys было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Judith Elizabetha Wylley, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Goodale 8 ноября 1590 г. в местности деннингтон, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла декабрь 1610 г. в местности деннингтон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Artys se bautizó el 4 de mayo de 1570, en Westhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Artys, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Judith Elizabetha Wylley, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Robert Goodale el 8 de noviembre de 1590, en Dennington, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en diciembre de 1610, en Dennington, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 40 años.
